en.extract:
The yellow-throated apalis (Apalis flavigularis) is a passerine bird in the family Cisticolidae. It is endemic to Malawi.  It was formerly considered a subspecies of the bar-throated apalis.
Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest and subtropical or tropical moist montane forest. It is threatened by habitat loss.

The '''yellow-throated apalis''' ('''''Apalis flavigularis''''') is a [[passerine]] bird in the family [[Cisticolidae]]. It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to [[Malawi]].  It was formerly considered a subspecies of the [[bar-throated apalis]].

Its natural [[habitat]]s are [[subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest]] and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]. It is threatened by [[habitat loss]].
